changes.reason: Batch D one-family charm-wall breakaway expansion. Current best base uses 1.0 and the profile remains the cleanest convex edge: 4 trades, $939.1 PnL, $583/hour, no slow trades, and worst MAE -346.8. Moving to the seed/grid value 1.5 tests whether stricter higher-high confirmation preserves the edge while reducing weak continuation admission risk.

Pair stricter higher-high confirmation with the prior successful higher-low support threshold. Current 0.5 is loose; 1.0 requires actual support lift without touching global structure or option-confirmed routing.

Use the Batch D seed value to test whether the 12-point max target is overexposing rare wall-breakaway entries. The profile has high MFE, but the risk-adjusted objective should verify whether a 6-point cap improves realization efficiency without destroying convex payoff.

expected_effect: This should keep charm_wall_breakaway_long as a high-convexity VALID_0DTE profile while testing tighter structural admission and a smaller max TP envelope. Expected benefit is better risk-adjusted efficiency and no increase in MAE or slow exposure. Main tradeoff is under-monetizing the rare 12-point wall-breakaway winners; reject if profile PnL/hour falls below about 225, total net profit drops more than 2% from run_044, or completed trades fall more than 10% without material objective improvement.
label: promote
rationale: Choose Batch D because the base run_044 is the score leader and the latest run_049 is a rejected ADX-period branch, so the next valid experiment should be profile-specific rather than another global entry perturbation. The charm_wall_breakaway_long edge is small-sample but very efficient and protected by the strategist plan. This is a legal one-family test using Batch D keys, avoids missing/stale 0DTE behavior, avoids broad veto tightening, and does not repeat the recently rejected VWAP-cap geometry or call-wall TP compression experiments.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long contributes only 4 VALID_0DTE trades but $939.1 PnL, $583/hour, median MFE 293.6, no slow trades, and worst MAE -346.8. The profile may support a more structure-confirmed admission branch without disturbing other VALID_0DTE profiles.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Wall Structure-Confirmed Convex Branch
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Small sample risk is high; stricter structure plus TP cap can remove or underpay the rare convex winners that make the profile valuable.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall breakaway with require-breakout true, price above charm wall, and ES higher-high/higher-low support confirmation.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Profile PnL/exposure remains >=225, total net profit >=98% of run_044, slow_12h remains 0, completed trades drop <=10% unless objective improves materially, and global worst MAE does not worsen.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: The worst current tail behavior is path-dependent in pullback_long and charm_pin_pullback_long. Static broad filters like RSI-slope floors and setup >=50 retain too little PnL, while setup >=45 is promising but not available in the active legal family set.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Early-Progress Heat Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit delayed green recoveries or block protected fast extended-VWAP winners unless validated against protected winner UTCs and out-of-sample slices.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: pullback_long or charm_pin_pullback_long entry that fails to make meaningful early positive progress before adverse expansion.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Remove slow >=12h trades, reduce worst MAE toward <= -513, retain >=90% combined pullback/charm-pin PnL, retain >=95% protected fast winners, and keep total score flat or better.
